items.title: China’s tech rise is creating a new kind of tourism
items.summary: Foreign visitors are flocking to China’s factories and AI startups in search of the next technological breakthrough.

items.title: U.S. companies have an AI problem. Indian IT wants to be the solution
items.summary: As firms find ROI elusive, India’s tech giants are betting they can fill the AI “deployment gap” for U.S. clients, before automation eats their own back-office business.

items.title: The agentic divide: Why “good enough” AI isn’t enough to survive the new economy
items.summary: A widening gap in agent quality is creating a two-tier system where well-resourced firms scale infinitely while small players are trapped by high-friction, "low-trust" tools.

items.title: U.S. versus China: Can open-source beat OpenAI?
items.summary: Join Jules Polonetsky, Chief Executive Officer, Future of Privacy Forum, in conversation with Itika Sharma Punit, Deputy Editor, Rest of World on April 17, 2026

items.title: AI is minting new billionaires, and workers want their share
items.summary: The Samsung labor showdown in South Korea reflects global concerns about who benefits from the AI industry, and how the wealth being created should be shared.

items.title: What AI race? China and U.S. AI worlds are tightly connected
items.summary: Despite geopolitical tensions, Chinese and American AI industries remain intertwined through research networks, collaboration, and a shared cultural identity.

items.title: Pushing back from Big Tech: Africa’s hard road to AI sovereignty
items.summary: The continent's biggest tech economies want to own their AI future. The infrastructure they need still belongs to Big Tech.

items.title: The UAE’s OPEC exit frees up oil wealth as it bets big on AI
Silicon Valley keeps misreading China’s role in tech
items.summary: The heads of Tesla, Apple, and Nvidia regard Chinese companies as peers, not enemies, unlike most other Silicon Valley founders.

items.title: The Filipino virtual assistants behind LinkedIn’s “thought leadership” content mill
items.summary: For $7 an hour, virtual assistants use AI tools to write LinkedIn posts and comments on behalf of Western executives.

items.title: India’s VCs are beating American investors at home
items.summary: A decade after U.S. venture capital firms fueled India’s startup boom, local investors are now dominating deals.

items.title: Can we really keep kids safe online?
items.summary: Future of Privacy Forum CEO Jules Polonetsky says protecting minors online requires more than just restrictions and parental controls.
